فَمَا لَكُمْ كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ- what's wrong with you? How do you make your decisions? This is another powerful point in the ayah: guidance is all about making decisions. The proof of someone committed to guidance is that they make the right kinds of decisions.

translation: And most of them follow not except assumption. Indeed, assumption avails not against the truth at all. Indeed, Allah is Knowing of what they do
commentary:
وَمَا يَتَّبِعُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ إِلَّا ظَنًّا ۚ إِنَّ ٱلظَّنَّ لَا يُغْنِى مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ شَيْـًٔا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا يَفْعَلُونَ

وَمَا يَتَّبِعُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ إِلَّا ظَنًّا- and most of them don't pursue anything except confident assumptions. إِنَّ ٱلظَّنَّ لَا يُغْنِى مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ شَيْـًٔا- no doubt about it, the confident assumption is not going to benefit you against the truth in any way, shape or form.

The verb يُغْنِى means to make someone free of need, to make someone independent. So the assumption is not going to make you free of need of the truth. It's not going to make you independent of the truth, as if you don't need it anymore because you're so confident in your assumption. Your confidence is not going to alleviate you from the need for truth. It's not going to save you.

This is what usually happens when somebody's really confident in their position, and Allah's ﷻ response to them is: your confidence doesn't change reality.

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا يَفْعَلُونَ- no doubt that Allah ﷻ is fully knowledgeable of what they are doing. Notice the word يَفْعَلُونَ is used here - mindless action. The other word for action in Arabic is يَعْمَلُونَ, which implies thoughtful, conscious action. But يَفْعَلُونَ is used because they've just made an assumption and they're acting on it - it's not based on real knowledge, it's not conscious.

translation: And it was not [possible] for this Qur'an to be produced by other than Allah, but [it is] a confirmation of what was before it and a detailed explanation of the [former] Scripture, about which there is no doubt, from the Lord of the worlds
commentary:
وَمَا كَانَ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانُ أَن يُفْتَرَىٰ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَـٰكِن تَصْدِيقَ ٱلَّذِى بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَتَفْصِيلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ مِن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ

Now that the foundations of their faith have been shattered, Allah ﷻ turns to address the actual revelation itself.

وَمَا كَانَ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانُ أَن يُفْتَرَىٰ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ- this Quran has never been something that can be made up, that's been made up by anyone other than Allah ﷻ. وَلَـٰكِن تَصْدِيقَ ٱلَّذِى بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ- however, this is what it is: a confirmation of what is right before it, meaning the previous revelations. It comes as a confirmation of the revelations that preceded it, the scriptures given to the People of the Book.

وَتَفْصِيلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ - and it's an explanation, a detailed exposition of the Book. The word تَفْصِيلَ (tafseel) also means previous revelation. لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ- no doubt whatsoever in that fact مِن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ- it is from the Master of all the worlds, from the Master of all nations.

translation: Or do they say [about the Prophet], "He invented it?" Say, "Then bring forth a surah like it and call upon [for assistance] whomever you can besides Allah, if you should be truthful
commentary:
أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ ۖ قُلْ فَأْتُوا۟ بِسُورَةٍ مِّثْلِهِۦ وَٱدْعُوا۟ مَنِ ٱسْتَطَعْتُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ

أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ - or is it that they're saying he made it up himself? 

قُلْ فَأْتُوا۟ بِسُورَةٍ مِّثْلِهِۦ- then bring a surah like it. Notice it says مِّثْلِهِۦ- something like it, similar to it, in the same category as it. There's an extra مِن added: bring me a surah from somewhere within even a mile radius of this. This started as an early challenge in the Quran.

The challenge gets more insulting over time. Earlier, the challenge was to bring ten surahs. That proved too hard. Okay, so clearly you haven't been able to bring ten surahs like it - how about you bring just one? And now: how about you bring something even close, something remotely similar? This was already an insult because the challenge before asked for ten complete surahs.

وَٱدْعُوا۟ مَنِ ٱسْتَطَعْتُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ- and call whoever you can find other than Allah ﷻ. Get help from rabbis, scholars, poets, anyone. إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ- if in fact you are truthful in your claim that this was made up by a human being.

translation: Rather, they have denied that which they encompass not in knowledge and whose interpretation has not yet come to them. Thus did those before them deny. Then observe how was the end of the wrongdoers
commentary:
بَلْ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِمَا لَمْ يُحِيطُوا۟ بِعِلْمِهِۦ وَلَمَّا يَأْتِهِمْ تَأْوِيلُهُۥ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۖ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ

Rather, the fact of the matter is they have lied against what they couldn't encompass with their knowledge. The phrase بِمَا لَمْ يُحِيطُوا۟ بِعِلْمِهِۦ (what they couldn't encircle with their knowledge) captures something profound about the nature of their rejection. They deny something they haven't even taken the time to understand. Their rationale becomes: "How can it be true if I don't know it?"

Allah ﷻ then adds وَلَمَّا يَأْتِهِمْ تَأْوِيلُهُۥ (and whose interpretation hasn't yet come). This is critical because these people kept demanding punishment, asking repeatedly: "Where is this punishment you're warning us about?" Allah ﷻ is saying they don't believe precisely because they haven't seen the punishment yet. The manifestation, the تَأْوِيل, hasn't arrived. That's their entire reason for disbelief—they want to see consequences first.

Then comes the historical perspective: كَذَٰلِكَ كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ (That is the course of action people before them had taken as well). This pattern of denial isn't new. Previous nations did exactly the same thing. And then the command: فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُينَ (Just look for yourselves—what was the outcome of those who did wrong?). History itself becomes the evidence. The ruins, the stories, the consequences—they're all there for anyone willing to observe.

translation: And of them are those who believe in it, and of them are those who do not believe in it. And your Lord is most knowing of the corrupters
commentary:
وَمِنْهُم مَّن يُؤْمِنُ بِهِۦ وَمِنْهُم مَّن لَّا يُؤْمِنُ بِهِۦ ۚ وَرَبُّكَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ

Among them there will be someone who believes in it, and among them there will be someone who does not believe in it. This ayah captures both present reality and future possibility. They're not a completely hopeless case. There are those who will eventually come to faith, and those who won't. Both the present and future are captured in this statement.

وَرَبُّكَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (And your Master knows better, is completely aware of those who are causes of corruption). The word المُفْسِدِينَ refers to those who actively spread corruption. Allah ﷻ doesn't just know who rejects—He knows who actively works to corrupt and mislead others. This knowledge belongs to Him alone.

translation: And if they deny you, [O Muhammad], then say, "For me are my deeds, and for you are your deeds. You are disassociated from what I do, and I am disassociated from what you do
commentary:
وَإِن كَذَّبُوكَ فَقُل لِّى عَمَلِى وَلَكُمْ عَمَلُكُمْ ۖ أَنتُم بَرِيٓـُٔونَ مِمَّآ أَعْمَلُ وَأَنَا۠ بَرِىٓءٌ مِّمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ

If they continue to lie against you, here's what you say: لِّى عَمَلِى وَلَكُمْ عَمَلُكُمْ (My deeds are mine and only mine, and your deeds are only yours). This is a declaration of complete separation of accountability. I'm not going to pay for your deeds, and you're not going to pay for mine.

أَنتُم بَرِيٓـُٔونَ مِمَّآ أَعْمَلُ وَأَنَا۠ بَرِىٓءٌ مِّمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (You are completely disconnected, disassociated from what I'm up to, and I am completely disconnected from whatever you people are up to). The word بَرِىٓءٌ means to be free from, to disassociate entirely.

This is actually a precursor to what we already read in Surah At-Tawbah. In that surah, there was a political and social form of disassociation—a severing of contracts and agreements after four months. But here in Mecca, this is the ideological form of disassociation. This is about belief, about faith. Two different contexts, two different forms of bara'ah (disassociation).

translation: And among them are those who listen to you. But can you cause the deaf to hear, although they will not use reason
commentary:
وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَسْتَمِعُونَ إِلَيْكَ ۚ أَفَأَنتَ تُسْمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ وَلَوْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ

Among them there are also those who come and act like they're really listening to you. The phrase يَسْتَمِعُونَ إِلَيْكَ isn't praise—it's actually sarcasm. They show up, they appear attentive, but what follows reveals the reality.

أَفَأَنتَ تُسْمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ (Are you going to make the deaf person capable of hearing?). Even if they're physically present, even if they appear to be listening, they're deaf to the message. And then the devastating addition: وَلَوْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ (even if they don't apply their intellect).

This was actually a tactic used by some of the disbelievers. They would come, sit through the entire message, listen carefully, and then go back to their people and say: "I listened. If it was worth believing, I would have taken it. There's nothing there. It's not a big deal." They weaponized their listening to dismiss the message with authority.

translation: And among them are those who look at you. But can you guide the blind although they will not [attempt to] see
commentary:
وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَنظُرُ إِلَيْكَ ۚ أَفَأَنتَ تَهْدِى ٱلْعُمْىَ وَلَوْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يُبْصِرُونَ

And then there is someone who comes and stares at you. The word يَنظُرُ carries weight here—this is about looking in an insulting way. This was one of the tactics used by the disbelievers that we learn more about in later Makkan surahs. You don't have to insult someone by responding with insults. You can insult them just by the way you look at them. People can look angry, dismissive, mocking—all without saying a word.

أَفَأَنتَ تَهْدِى ٱلْعُمْىَ (Are you going to be guiding the blind?). This person is staring so intently, using his eyes so much, yet the fact is he has no eyes. He cannot see. وَلَوْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يُبْصِرُونَ (even if they cannot see whatsoever). The physical sight is there, but the insight, the basirah, is completely absent.

translation: Indeed, Allah does not wrong the people at all, but it is the people who are wronging themselves
commentary:
إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ ٱلنَّاسَ شَيْـًٔا وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلنَّاسَ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ

No doubt about it: Allah ﷻ does not wrong anybody in any way, shape or form. لَا يَظْلِمُ ٱلنَّاسَ شَيْـًٔا—not even the smallest thing, not even an atom's weight of wrong.

وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلنَّاسَ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (However, people—they're the ones who wrong their own selves). Allah ﷻ is not wronging them by not sending the punishment immediately or by not sending the miracle they demand. They are the criminals in this scenario. They wrong themselves by their rejection, by their mockery, by their delay in accepting the truth.

translation: And on the Day when He will gather them, [it will be] as if they had not remained [in the world] but an hour of the day, [and] they will know each other. Those will have lost who denied the meeting with Allah and were not guided
commentary:
وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ كَأَن لَّمْ يَلْبَثُوٓا۟ إِلَّا سَاعَةً مِّنَ ٱلنَّهَارِ يَتَعَارَفُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ قَدْ خَسِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِلِقَآءِ ٱللَّهِ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُهْتَدِينَ

They kept asking: "Bring the punishment, bring the punishment! Why is it taking so long?" And the Day that He does gather them, they're going to say they didn't stay in the world—they didn't remain there—except maybe an hour of the day.

كَأَن لَّمْ يَلْبَثُوٓا۟ إِلَّا سَاعَةً مِّنَ ٱلنَّهَارِ (as though they had stayed only an hour of the day). Whether it was nighttime or daytime, an hour goes by quickly when you're busy. Life itself will feel like it was over just like that—in a flash.

يَتَعَارَفُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ (They'll be recognizing each other). On Judgment Day, these same disbelievers—the tribal leaders, the community figures—will just be beginning to recognize one another. It's as if they're waking up to a new reality.

قَدْ خَسِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِلِقَآءِ ٱللَّهِ (Those who had lied against the meeting of Allah have already fallen into loss). The transaction is complete. The deal is done. They've lost everything.

وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُهْتَدِينَ (And they were not going to be ones committed to guidance). Past tense, but it applies to their entire trajectory. They were never on the path of guidance, never committed to it.

translation: And whether We show you some of what We promise them, [O Muhammad], or We take you in death, to Us is their return; then, [either way], Allah is a witness concerning what they are doing
commentary:
وَإِمَّا نُرِيَنَّكَ بَعْضَ ٱلَّذِى نَعِدُهُمْ أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ فَإِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ ثُمَّ ٱللَّهُ شَهِيدٌ عَلَىٰ مَا يَفْعَلُونَ

They don't believe because the interpretation, the manifestation of what they're asking for hasn't happened yet. So Allah ﷻ says to the Messenger ﷺ: If it was the case that We would show you some of what We've promised them—or if We were to take you away, call you back to Us—either way, فَإِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ (they have to return back to Us).

Whether you see it or not, Messenger of Allah ﷺ, whether the punishment comes in your lifetime or after you've passed, their return is to Us. ثُمَّ ٱللَّهُ شَهِيدٌ عَلَىٰ مَا يَفْعَلُونَ (Then Allah Himself, in the end, is a witness over whatever mindless acts they're involved in). This is a powerful consolation for the Prophet ﷺ. Your job is to deliver. The outcome, the timing, the consequences—all of that belongs to Allah ﷻ alone.

translation: And for every nation is a messenger. So when their messenger comes, it will be judged between them in justice, and they will not be wronged
commentary:
وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَّسُولٌ ۖ فَإِذَا جَآءَ رَسُولُهُمْ قُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْقِسْطِ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ

Every nation has had, and every nation has, a messenger. وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَّسُولٌ—this is the pattern throughout history. No people have been left without guidance.

فَإِذَا جَآءَ رَسُولُهُمْ قُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْقِسْطِ (Whenever a messenger comes to them, their matter is decided with justice). There's a warning embedded here. The messenger has come to you. The only thing ahead of you now is Allah's verdict with justice.

وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (And they're not going to be wronged). That judgment came eventually in Surah At-Tawbah. We're looking back at this from a post-Tawbah perspective, coming out of Tawbah into Yunus. The justice Allah ﷻ promised has already begun to unfold in history.

translation: And they say, "When is [the fulfillment of] this promise, if you should be truthful
commentary:
وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَـٰذَا ٱلْوَعْدُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ

And they would say: "When is this promise that you've been promising? When is this going to take place—if in fact you've got something, some truth you're holding onto?" This is their mocking challenge, repeated throughout the Quran. They treat the promise of consequences as something theoretical, something that will never materialize.

translation: Say, "I possess not for myself any harm or benefit except what Allah should will. For every nation is a [specified] term. When their time has come, then they will not remain behind an hour, nor will they precede [it]
commentary:
قُل لَّآ أَمْلِكُ لِنَفْسِى ضَرًّا وَلَا نَفْعًا إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۗ لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلٌ ۚ إِذَا جَآءَ أَجَلُهُمْ فَلَا يَسْتَـْٔخِرُونَ سَاعَةً ۖ وَلَا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ

Tell them: "I don't control even harm or benefit for my own self. I'm not in possession of that—إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ (except what Allah wills)."

This connects to the concept of shafa'ah (intercession). There are two kinds: true and false. True intercession exists—there is legitimate shafa'ah. But most people have fallen into shirk because of illegitimate concepts of intercession. The Messenger ﷺ is a messenger, and Muslims do believe there is intercession. But there's a danger: there's a correct way of accepting the Messenger ﷺ as an intercessor, and an incorrect way. This is where many Muslims have gone astray.

Our love for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ is natural. Allah ﷻ placed it in our hearts. One of the most beautiful ayahs about the love of the Messenger ﷺ is in Surah Al-Hujurat: Allah ﷻ beautified iman in your hearts. And what does that ayah deal with? Obeying the messenger, respecting the messenger, lowering your voice out of love and regard for the messenger. That's how Allah ﷻ beautified iman in your hearts.

But this subject has been hijacked. On one hand, there are groups who go to extremes with it. On the other hand, another group refuses to even talk about it—because if you talk about it, you must be from that first group. We've gone to such extremes that the subject itself has become taboo, when it's actually one of the central matters of our religion.

Here's what you should never believe about the Messenger ﷺ: something that Allah ﷻ told him to say. "I have no authority to benefit myself or even harm myself." He's not in control of harming or benefiting anyone. He doesn't have that authority. This is what Allah ﷻ told him to tell people—and not just the enemies of Islam. The Prophet ﷺ told his own daughter, Fatimah (RA): "O Fatimah, daughter of Muhammad! Fear Allah. I will have no authority for you before Allah. I won't be able to help you." He said this to his own daughter.

So before we talk about the Messenger's shafa'ah, we should cleanse the corrupt concepts first. Get those out of our system. That's what we're reading about now.

The psychological pattern behind shirk: "I don't want to face God myself. I'm way too messed up to face God directly. I'd rather just be good to someone in between, someone who's already been really good to God. He can deal with Him. I'll just make this intermediary happy." This is what Christians do—they love Jesus (AS). When they talk about God, they talk about punishments. When they talk about Jesus (AS), they talk about mercy. God destroyed nations, God punished, God gives law and punishment—that's what the sermon tells you. Jesus (AS) came as mercy. He's God's son, and God loves His son so much that if you please His son, you're with His son, then you're good to go.

What have Muslims done with that concept, even to this day? It's not just a historical thing—it's a psychological disease, a disorder. You'll have someone involved in all kinds of impermissible businesses, violating all the laws of Allah ﷻ regarding family and inheritance—but when it comes to singing nasheed about the Messenger ﷺ, he'll do it all night. Out of claimed love, they'll recite praises, but those lyrics sometimes cross dangerous lines. Some of those lyrics contain statements that are straight-up shirk. I've heard lyrics on the radio where they say things like "God came down as Mustafa" (ﷺ). This is how you celebrate the Prophet ﷺ? By undermining everything he stood for?

The problem with unqualified statements: "You're the only one whose praises are being sung. You're the only one who will save us." You can't just say that without qualification. A scholar can say it properly, with the right context and understanding—but when a common person hears it, do they have the scholarly background to know where to draw the line? No. It creates confusion and misunderstanding.

Even expressions of love need understanding. If I miss my grandmother who passed away and I say "I miss you, Grandma" while driving my car, that doesn't mean I think she's sitting next to me like a ghost. It just means I love her so much that I want to express that love. That's not a theological problem. But we've made it one.

When common audiences get hold of texts that were written for scholarly study, the danger of misinterpretation is way too high. That's where we've landed ourselves: in pointless online debates between ignorant people about issues they don't understand.

If somebody genuinely wants to learn, study and appreciate how to love the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, there's no better study than the Quran. No nasheed written will ever be better than Allah ﷻ praising His Messenger ﷺ. You genuinely want to develop love for the Messenger ﷺ? Study the Quran. We're fighting over how much we love the Messenger ﷺ without even knowing the Quran's teachings about him.

لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلٌ (For every nation there is a deadline). إِذَا جَآءَ أَجَلُهُمْ فَلَا يَسْتَـْٔخِرُونَ سَاعَةً ۖ وَلَا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ (When the deadline arrives, they are not able to postpone it, put it off or make it later by a single moment, by a single hour. Nor are they able to bring it earlier than expected).

Why is "advancing it" mentioned? I understand they'd want to delay punishment—but which nation would want to advance it? Because they're asking for it now. When it actually comes, they'll want to delay it. And when it doesn't come, they mock and say "bring it earlier." Both are mentioned because both capture their contradictory attitudes.

translation: Say, "Have you considered: if His punishment should come to you by night or by day - for which [aspect] of it would the criminals be impatient
commentary:
قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَتَىٰكُمْ عَذَابُهُۥ بَيَـٰتًا أَوْ نَهَارًا مَّاذَا يَسْتَعْجِلُ مِنْهُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ

Tell them: "Have you considered—if His punishment came and attacked you in the middle of the night when you're at home, or in the middle of the day?" 

مَّاذَا يَسْتَعْجِلُ مِنْهُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ (What preparation qualifies these criminals to rush towards it so much? What is it you have ready? Why are you so confident you can handle it?) You're saying "bring it"—but what makes you think you're prepared?

translation: Then is it that when it has [actually] occurred you will believe in it? Now? And you were [once] for it impatient
commentary:
أَثُمَّ إِذَا مَا وَقَعَ ءَامَنتُم بِهِۦٓ ۚ ءَآلْـَٔـٰنَ وَقَدْ كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ

And then at the very moment that it drops, that it takes place, that it happens—NOW you're believing in it?

The word ءَآلْـَٔـٰنَ (now) is separated from all the rest of the speech. If I was writing this in English, I would write multiple exclamation marks: "NOW?! NOW you believe?!" 

وَقَدْ كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (And you used to be the ones asking for it, rushing it: "Bring it! Bring it! Where did it go? How come it hasn't come yet?") What happened to your tongue now?

translation: Then it will be said to those who had wronged, "Taste the punishment of eternity; are you being recompensed except for what you used to earn
commentary:
ثُمَّ قِيلَ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلْخُلْدِ هَلْ تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ

Then it will be said to those who did wrong: "Taste the punishment of permanence." ذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلْخُلْدِ—that taste you're getting right now? It's not going anywhere. It's not going to get better. You're not going to get used to it. It is permanent.

هَلْ تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ (Have you been compensated with anything other than what you earned yourselves?)

translation: And they ask information of you, [O Muhammad], "Is it true?" Say, "Yes, by my Lord. Indeed, it is truth; and you will not cause failure [to Allah]
commentary:
۞ وَيَسْتَنۢبِـُٔونَكَ أَحَقٌّ هُوَ ۖ قُلْ إِى وَرَبِّىٓ إِنَّهُۥ لَحَقٌّ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ

When they heard some of the serious warnings, some of them would have a crisis of faith, a moment of doubt about their disbelief. They would come to the Prophet ﷺ—the word يَسْتَنۢبِـُٔونَكَ comes from naba', meaning news. They come seeking news, asking seriously for a moment: أَحَقٌّ هُوَ (Is this for real? Really, it is?)

Notice the sentence structure. Normally you'd say هُوَ حَقٌّ (it is true), but they're so shocked that the word order changes: أَحَقٌّ هُوَ (Real, it is? Seriously?).

The response: قُلْ إِى (Say: Yes!). How do you normally say yes in Arabic? نعم. But when you yell at somebody and say YES emphatically, authoritatively, you say إِى. Just إِى. Of course. Yes. It's an authoritative yes.

وَرَبِّىٓ (And I swear by my Master). This is one of the few instances in the Quran where the Messenger ﷺ is told to swear by Allah ﷻ. The swearing is for emphasis. The oath is for emphasis.

إِنَّهُۥ لَحَقٌّ (It truly is. It really is the truth). The إِنَّ is for emphasis. The ل in لَحَقٌّ is for emphasis. You can't say this statement normally—إِى وَرَبِّىٓ إِنَّهُۥ لَحَقٌّ. You can't just say it casually. This is too heavy for you to say it lightly.

وَمَآ أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ (And you're not going to be ones to overpower this message. You're not going to bring this message to its knees). Someone made incapable, someone brought to their knees—that's what mu'jizeen means. This is a review of Surah At-Tawbah where Allah ﷻ said: وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّكُمْ غَيْرُ مُعْجِزِى ٱللَّهِ (You had better know you're not going to be overpowering Allah). And way back in Mecca, Allah ﷻ already said it: You're not going to overpower this message.

translation: And if each soul that wronged had everything on earth, it would offer it in ransom. And they will confide regret when they see the punishment; and they will be judged in justice, and they will not be wronged
commentary:
وَلَوْ أَنَّ لِكُلِّ نَفْسٍ ظَلَمَتْ مَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ لَٱفْتَدَتْ بِهِۦ ۗ وَأَسَرُّوا۟ ٱلنَّدَامَةَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ۖ وَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۚ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ

If every person that did wrong possessed everything that's on the earth—مَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ. Why is this phrase used, and not just "all the treasures of the earth"? Because there are so many treasures in the world that we know about, and so many we don't. The known and unknown treasures together.

If the wrongdoer possessed all of that, لَٱفْتَدَتْ بِهِۦ (he would give it in ransom for his own self). At that point, shafa'ah is canceled out. Nobody is going to come and pay for your crimes. Nobody is going to shield you from punishment. Maybe you can pay your way out. Maybe you can pay the fine, the penalty. If you had the entire world with all its known and unknown treasures to give—you would give it all.

وَأَسَرُّوا۟ ٱلنَّدَامَةَ (And while they're giving it, they're hiding their shame). This is criminal psychology. When a criminal pays a fine, he feels like he no longer has reason to be guilty: "I paid for my crime, right?" And when a criminal is testifying in court, if he looks guilty, the jury looks at that and says "He IS guilty." So he has to try to hide his guilt. The criminal does his best to hide his nadamah (regret).

لَمَّا رَأَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ (when they saw the punishment)—because they think they might still have a defense.

وَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْقِسْطِ (And their matter will have been decided with justice). وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (And they're not going to be wronged).

translation: Unquestionably, to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Unquestionably, the promise of Allah is truth, but most of them do not know
commentary:
أَلَآ إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ

أَلَآ (You had better know)—this word carries emphasis and certainty. إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ (Allah alone owns whatever is in the skies and the earth).

أَلَآ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ (You had better know—the promise of Allah is true). The promise being highlighted throughout these ayahs is the warning of Judgment Day. It's real. It's coming. It's certain.

وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (However, most of them—they don't even know. They have no idea). The knowledge of this reality, this promise, this warning—most people remain completely unaware.

May Allah ﷻ make us people who truly know, and help other people come to know as well.

translation: He gives life and causes death, and to Him you will be returned
commentary:
**هُوَ يُحْىِۦ وَيُمِيتُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ**

*He is the One who gives life and causes death, and to Him alone you will all be returned.*

This ayah establishes a fundamental truth about Allah's ﷻ absolute authority over life and death. The transition here is particularly beautiful. The previous passage discussed sins as diseases of the heart, with shirk being the most devastating spiritual illness. Now, having addressed death and disease, Allah ﷻ transitions to giving life back to hearts through revelation. This passage occupies a central position in the entire surah, answering a critical question: if we cannot rely on false intercessors, what should we rely upon? The answer is Allah's ﷻ promises and His words of revelation—the Quran.

translation: O mankind, there has to come to you instruction from your Lord and healing for what is in the breasts and guidance and mercy for the believers
commentary:
**يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم مَّوْعِظَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَشِفَآءٌ لِّمَا فِى ٱلصُّدُورِ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ**

*O humanity! There has come to you a counsel from your Master, and a healing for what is in the chests, and guidance and mercy for the believers.*

### The Four Qualities of the Quran

Allah ﷻ addresses all of humanity, not just believers, introducing the Quran through four essential qualities.

**First: Maw'idhah—Heart-Penetrating Counsel**

The word مَوْعِظَة refers to advice that penetrates deep into the heart at precisely the right moment. We receive advice constantly—from friends, parents, coworkers—but most of it goes in one ear and out the other. However, there are moments when your heart is open and counsel reaches deep inside, staying with you permanently. This is maw'idhah.

Notice the emphasis: مَوْعِظَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ—counsel from your Master. The word رَبّ itself carries connotations of nurturing and care, used with the possessive "your" to emphasize the personal relationship. This emotionally charged counsel comes from the One who knows you best, who created you, who cares for you. We seek counsel when we face problems in life, and Allah ﷻ is teaching us that when issues arise, we should turn to His Book for guidance.

**Second: Shifā'—Healing for the Chests**

The disease exists in the heart, and now the medicine has penetrated inside. Consider the imagery: when medicine enters the affected area of the body, it immediately begins healing and taking effect. The word used is وَشِفَآءٌ لِّمَا فِى ٱلصُّدُورِ—healing for what is in the chests (sudoor), not just the hearts (qulub). Why the distinction?

The صَدْر (chest) is the area surrounding the قَلْب (heart). Allah ﷻ doesn't restrict the cure only to the heart itself but expands it to everything surrounding the heart. This encompasses the realm of waswasah—the whispers and doubts that circle around the heart, the domain where Shaytan operates. The Quran cures not just the heart but the entire spiritual environment around it.

However, for this healing to work, the heart must be receptive. We've already learned in this surah that some people will never accept this counsel because they refuse to let it enter. You must first accept that this is genuine counsel, then allow it to penetrate, and only then will the cure begin.

Some people wonder: "I've committed so many sins—how can my heart ever be clean?" These ayahs provide the answer. Allow Allah's ﷻ counsel in, and the diseases, the filth accumulated over time, will begin to be cleansed.

**Third: Hudā—Guidance**

Once you accept the advice and it begins curing you, you become ready to receive its instructions. Think of visiting a doctor: first, they inject medicine or provide pain relief. Once you feel some relief, they give you instructions—follow this prescription, avoid these foods, take this medicine at these times. That instruction is هُدًى.

After the healing begins, the Quran provides clear guidance on how to live, what to do and what to avoid. This guidance maintains and builds upon the healing that has already commenced.

**Fourth: Rahmah—Mercy for the Believers**

When you follow those instructions, you feel better. Only then do you truly realize what a mercy this revelation is. The blessing becomes fully apparent. This final quality is specifically designated لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (for the believers) because they are the ones who complete the entire process: accepting the counsel, allowing it to heal them, following its guidance, and thereby experiencing its full mercy.

### A Universal Address

Something remarkable about this ayah: Allah ﷻ addresses يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ (O humanity). He's speaking to the mushrikoon, the very people the first half of this surah has been scolding. Why? Because the perception of the Quran should be determined by the Quran itself. Let the Quran introduce itself to people.

In modern media, the Quran has been demonized. Most non-Muslims encounter frightening, negative portrayals. Just recently, I sat next to an engineer on a flight who confessed that when he thinks of the Quran, he thinks of something scary, something evil, something with secret agendas—the exact opposite of what Allah ﷻ intends.

When non-Muslims hear about the Quran, the message should be so widespread, so commonly discussed, that it becomes unavoidable. That's our responsibility as Muslims—to share this message broadly and let people hear what the Quran says about itself.

translation: Say, "In the bounty of Allah and in His mercy - in that let them rejoice; it is better than what they accumulate
commentary:
**قُلْ بِفَضْلِ ٱللَّهِ وَبِرَحْمَتِهِۦ فَبِذَٰلِكَ فَلْيَفْرَحُوا۟ هُوَ خَيْرٌ مِّمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ**

*Tell them: It is exclusively by the favor of Allah and by His mercy—so in that, let them rejoice. It is better than everything they gather.*

### The Only Reason to Rejoice

Allah ﷻ commands the Prophet ﷺ to tell people that receiving the Quran is entirely by Allah's ﷻ special favor and mercy. Remember the earlier ayah: "If Allah willed, I would never have recited it to you, nor would He have made it known to you." You would never have been exposed to the Quran if Allah ﷻ hadn't willed it. Now that you've received such a beautiful gift, you should be overjoyed.

The phrase فَبِذَٰلِكَ فَلْيَفْرَحُوا۟ (so in that, let them rejoice) is carefully constructed. The word فَرَح typically describes inappropriate joy—celebrating something trivial or worldly. But Allah ﷻ provides one legitimate reason for this kind of overwhelming joy: the revelation of the Quran. This is the only thing worthy of such celebration.

### Better Than Everything You Collect

هُوَ خَيْرٌ مِّمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ—It is better than everything they gather. The word يَجْمَعُونَ (they gather) is profound. When people love something, they collect it. Children gather toys, pencils, then as they grow older, video games. Adults gather money, possessions, collections of various things. People treasure their collections, proudly showing them to visitors.

Every time something is added to a collection, there's a small celebration. "Another one!" Allah ﷻ says the Quran is better than all of these gatherings. The implication? Every time you learn a new ayah, every time you memorize and understand another portion of revelation, you should experience that same joy—even greater joy. "Alhamdulillah, another ayah! SubhanAllah, another insight!"

This reminds me of meeting an elderly Muslim man, a blind teacher in his sixties who used to live in Milwaukee. He moved there in the 1950s when there were only four Muslim families in the entire city. He described how they would pray Jumu'ah in someone's living room. Occasionally, they'd spot someone in a grocery store who looked like they might be from a Muslim country. They'd muster up courage, approach them, and say, "Assalamu Alaikum." Usually, they'd get confused looks, but sometimes—sometimes—the person would respond, "Wa'alaikum Assalam! You're Muslim!" They'd hug each other, exchange numbers, and that would be the highlight of their year. "We met another Muslim! It was amazing!"

Now there are multiple masajid, full congregations, no space for parking. But that love they had when finding even one other Muslim—that appreciation for something precious—has diminished. It's a similar sentiment with knowledge. When we become obsessed with acquiring vast amounts of knowledge, we lose appreciation for the small things, for the single ayah itself.

I remember when I first started learning Arabic and was taught about the mudāf and mudāf ilayh (possessive construction). The teacher gave us an assignment to find examples without looking at translations. I found four instances and felt amazed—"I can find this myself!" Then we prayed, and the imam was reciting, and I heard مَـٰلِكِ يَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ (Master of the Day of Judgment)—mudāf and mudāf ilayh together. I cried in sujood, making dua for my teacher. He helped me understand those two words in a way I never would have without him. Every single time I understand something new, without fail, I add to his reward.

This is the attitude we should have: celebration for every inch closer to understanding, every new word learned, every surah memorized. When you read a page and learn a word you didn't know before, then come back and read it again understanding what it means—that should be a celebration event.

Especially when studying parables in the Quran—the scholars have written volumes about them, but when you encounter an insight on a parable and see something beautiful, you just want to thank Allah ﷻ for the gift He's giving you. What a treasure.

translation: Say, "Have you seen what Allah has sent down to you of provision of which you have made [some] lawful and [some] unlawful?" Say, "Has Allah permitted you [to do so], or do you invent [something] about Allah
commentary:
**قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُم مَّآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ لَكُم مِّن رِّزْقٍ فَجَعَلْتُم مِّنْهُ حَرَامًا وَحَلَـٰلًا قُلْ ءَآللَّهُ أَذِنَ لَكُمْ ۖ أَمْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ تَفْتَرُونَ**

*Tell them: Have you considered what Allah has sent down to you as provision, and you have made from it harām and halāl? Say: Has Allah given you permission for this, or do you fabricate lies against Allah?*

### Who Decides What's Permissible?

Allah ﷻ poses a devastating question to those who make their own religious rulings. He provided the provision—the food, the resources. If He is the One who gives it, He alone has the right to decide what is permissible and what is forbidden. 

The rhetorical questions strike at the heart of the issue: ءَآللَّهُ أَذِنَ لَكُمْ (Did Allah give you permission?) أَمْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ تَفْتَرُونَ (Or are you fabricating against Allah?). There's no third option. Either Allah ﷻ authorized these rulings, or people are making them up and attributing them to Him—which is a grave lie.

This addresses the Arab practice of declaring certain livestock forbidden based on superstition, but the principle extends universally: no one has the authority to declare something halāl or harām without divine permission. This is Allah's ﷻ exclusive right as the Provider and Lawgiver.

translation: And what will be the supposition of those who invent falsehood about Allah on the Day of Resurrection? Indeed, Allah is full of bounty to the people, but most of them are not grateful
commentary:
**وَمَا ظَنُّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ**

*And what is the assumption of those who fabricate lies against Allah on the Day of Resurrection? Indeed, Allah is Possessor of bounty for humanity, but most of them are not grateful.*

### The Day of Reckoning

What confident assumption do these fabricators hold about the Day of Judgment? What do they think will happen when they face Allah ﷻ after attributing false rulings to Him? The question itself implies the severity of their situation—they haven't thought through the consequences of their actions.

Then comes a profound statement: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ (Indeed, Allah is Possessor of bounty for humanity). Why would you make such ill assumptions about Allah ﷻ? Why would you assume He doesn't have blessings for you? Why would you need to turn to someone else for intercession or rulings?

Allah ﷻ is saying: I am the Possessor of great favors upon people. I have abundance to give. You don't need intermediaries or fabricated permissions. وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ (But most of them are not grateful)—despite all these blessings, most people fail to show gratitude. They turn away from the Source of all goodness and create their own systems instead.

translation: And, [O Muhammad], you are not [engaged] in any matter or recite any of the Qur'an and you [people] do not do any deed except that We are witness over you when you are involved in it. And not absent from your Lord is any [part] of an atom's weight within the earth or within the heaven or [anything] smaller than that or greater but that it is in a clear register
commentary:
**وَمَا تَكُونُ فِى شَأْنٍ وَمَا تَتْلُوا۟ مِنْهُ مِن قُرْءَانٍ وَلَا تَعْمَلُونَ مِنْ عَمَلٍ إِلَّا كُنَّا عَلَيْكُمْ شُهُودًا إِذْ تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَا يَعْزُبُ عَن رَّبِّكَ مِن مِّثْقَالِ ذَرَّةٍ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَلَآ أَصْغَرَ مِن ذَٰلِكَ وَلَآ أَكْبَرَ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مُّبِينٍ**

*And you are never in any situation, and you never recite any portion of the Quran, and you people never do any deed except that We are witnesses over you when you are deeply engaged in it. And nothing—not even the weight of a speck—escapes your Master, neither in the earth nor in the sky, nor anything smaller than that or larger, except that it is in a clear Record.*

### Allah Witnesses Everything

For anyone who wants to serve Islam in any capacity, this is an ayah to memorize and wear upon your heart. Allah ﷻ addresses His Messenger ﷺ first, then extends the address to all believers.

**To the Prophet ﷺ:** You will never find yourself in any situation, you will never recite a single ayah from the Quran anywhere—

**To All Muslims:** And there is not a single deed that any of you will do—

إِلَّا كُنَّا عَلَيْكُمْ شُهُودًا (except that We are witnesses over you). Allah ﷻ witnesses over all things constantly, but here He says كُنَّا—We will be special witnesses, paying particular attention to your actions.

### The Meaning of Tufeedoon

The phrase إِذْ تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ uses the word تُفِيضُونَ, from the root ف-ي-ض, which means to overflow. When water pours beyond a container's capacity and spills out, that's fayd. When massive crowds pour out from one region during Hajj, heading to another location, that overwhelming flow is described as إفَاضَة.

What does this mean in context? When you are so completely immersed in an activity that you lose track of everything else—when you're having four-hour planning meetings, putting projects together, obsessing over details day and night, entirely engulfed in the work—إِذْ تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ (when you overflow into it). Allah ﷻ says: I am watching that level of dedication. I am appreciating that complete engagement.

### Nothing Escapes His Knowledge

وَمَا يَعْزُبُ عَن رَّبِّكَ (And nothing escapes your Master). The word يَعْزُبُ means to disappear from view, like watching someone walk away until they become so distant you can no longer see them, or throwing a pebble into a lake and watching it get smaller and smaller until it vanishes from sight.

Nothing disappears from Allah's ﷻ vision, no matter how far or how small: مِن مِّثْقَالِ ذَرَّةٍ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ (not the weight of a speck in the earth or in the sky) وَلَآ أَصْغَرَ مِن ذَٰلِكَ وَلَآ أَكْبَرَ (nor anything smaller than that or larger).

What is Allah ﷻ referring to? Not just physical specks in the universe, but the smallest deed you do for this deen will not go unappreciated. Allah ﷻ is telling us He will be a special witness to every tiny action taken for His sake.

إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مُّبِينٍ (Except that it is in a clear Record)—everything you do is recorded in a Book that clarifies and preserves all actions, leaving nothing unaccounted for.

translation: Unquestionably, [for] the allies of Allah there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve
commentary:
**أَلَآ إِنَّ أَوْلِيَآءَ ٱللَّهِ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ**

*Indeed, the close friends of Allah—there shall be no fear upon them, nor shall they grieve.*

### Becoming a Friend of Allah

Allah ﷻ just told us He appreciates everything we do for His sake. Now He introduces the concept of أَوْلِيَآءَ ٱللَّهِ—the close, protective friends of Allah ﷻ. These special people have no fear concerning the future and no grief about the past.

Who are these friends of Allah ﷻ? The concept might seem far beyond our reach, but Allah ﷻ immediately provides the definition in the next ayah, making it surprisingly accessible.

translation: Those who believed and were fearing Allah
commentary:
**ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ يَتَّقُونَ**

*Those who believe and maintain taqwa.*

### The Simple Definition

That's it. The friends of Allah ﷻ are those who believe and continuously maintain an attitude of taqwa in their lives. You can do that. You can be a wali of Allah ﷻ.

Why didn't Allah ﷻ make this an unrealistic expectation—requiring some impossibly high spiritual status? Because when friendship with Allah ﷻ becomes unrealistic, that's when people turn to someone else, saying, "He's so much better than us. He should be the wali. I should just work with him." The entire concept of seeking intercessors developed because people felt distanced from Allah ﷻ.

But Allah ﷻ says: No, I'm not asking you for much. You can be My wali. And I'm appreciating every little thing you're doing.

All it requires is genuine īmān and persistent taqwa—being conscious of Allah ﷻ, avoiding what displeases Him, doing what pleases Him. Maintain that, and you have the opportunity to become among the protective friends of Allah ﷻ, free from fear and grief.

translation: For them are good tidings in the worldly life and in the Hereafter. No change is there in the words of Allah. That is what is the great attainment
commentary:
لَهُمُ ٱلْبُشْرَىٰ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَفِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِكَلِمَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ

The ayah continues the description of the awliya of Allah ﷻ from the previous passage, declaring that they are the ones who receive البشرى - the ultimate good news, the best possible news - in worldly life and in the Hereafter. The word bushra captures both the announcement of good news and the gift itself, showing how Allah ﷻ brings them His gifts even in this worldly life.

Then comes a powerful declaration: لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِكَلِمَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ - there is no change possible whatsoever, under any circumstance, to the words of Allah ﷻ. This statement carries multiple layers of meaning. It refers to the promises Allah ﷻ has made to the believers, which are absolutely immutable. It also encompasses His revealed words, His decrees, and His divine will. Nothing and no one can alter what Allah ﷻ has established.

The ayah concludes with ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ- that, in fact, is the ultimate success. This comprehensive promise of good news in this life and the next, backed by the unchangeable word of Allah ﷻ, represents the greatest achievement any human being could aspire to.

translation: And let not their speech grieve you. Indeed, honor [due to power] belongs to Allah entirely. He is the Hearing, the Knowing
commentary:
وَلَا يَحْزُنكَ قَوْلُهُمْ ۘ إِنَّ ٱلْعِزَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا ۚ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ

After describing the believers and their rewards, Allah ﷻ addresses the Prophet ﷺ directly with a message of consolation. The opening command وَلَا يَحْزُنكَ قَوْلُهُمْ- "let not their words grieve you" - acknowledges the emotional burden that comes with delivering the message to those who reject it. The Prophet ﷺ is being told that their words, their rejections, their insults should not cause him grief.

The reason follows immediately: إِنَّ ٱلْعِزَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا- all glory, nobility, and authority belong to Allah ﷻ altogether. The word عزة encompasses honor, might, power, and invincibility. When the disbelievers speak against the message, they might appear powerful or influential, but the reality is that all true power rests exclusively with Allah ﷻ. Their opposition is ultimately meaningless because they oppose the One who possesses all authority.

The ayah concludes by reminding us that He is السميع العليم- the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing. He hears everything they say, and He knows the full context, their intentions, and what lies in their hearts. This knowledge should comfort the Prophet ﷺ and every believer who faces opposition.

translation: Unquestionably, to Allah belongs whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth. And those who invoke other than Allah do not [actually] follow [His] "partners." They follow not except assumption, and they are not but falsifying
commentary:
أَلَآ إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَمَا يَتَّبِعُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ شُرَكَآءَ ۚ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَخْرُصُونَ

The ayah opens with أَلَآ, an emphatic particle meaning "you had better know" or "indeed, certainly." It demands attention for what follows. What we typically see in the Quran is مَا فِى السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِى الْأَرْضِ- "whatever is in the heavens and the earth." But here the phrasing changes dramatically: مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ- "whoever is in the heavens and the earth."

This shift from "whatever" to "whoever" is deliberate and profound. Allah ﷻ is asserting His exclusive ownership over all conscious beings, all creatures with will and choice. This establishes the foundation for what comes next: if Allah ﷻ owns all conscious beings, then calling upon anyone other than Him makes no sense whatsoever.

The ayah then addresses the fundamental error of shirk: وَمَا يَتَّبِعُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ شُرَكَآءَ- "and those who call upon associates other than Allah are not following anything." They might think they're following deities, following tradition, following wisdom passed down through generations, but the reality is they're following nothing of substance.

The description becomes even more devastating: إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ- they follow nothing except assumption, conjecture, wishful thinking. They have no actual knowledge, no proof, no evidence. وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَخْرُصُونَ- and they're doing nothing but making wild guesses.

The word خرص appears several times in the Quran and refers to making assertions without knowledge, like someone looking at a palm tree and claiming they can tell you exactly how many dates are on it. It's pretending to know what cannot be known, speaking with confidence about matters of which one is completely ignorant.

translation: It is He who made for you the night to rest therein and the day, giving sight. Indeed in that are signs for a people who listen
commentary:
هُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ لِتَسْكُنُوا۟ فِيهِ وَٱلنَّهَارَ مُبْصِرًا ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ

After establishing His exclusive ownership and the baselessness of shirk, Allah ﷻ presents evidence from creation itself. He is the One who made the night for you لِتَسْكُنُوا۟ فِيهِ - so that you can become still in it, so you can rest. The word سكن means to cease motion, to become calm and tranquil. This is the purpose Allah ﷻ built into the night.

Then He made the day مُبْصِرًا- as a means by which you can see, as illuminating. Notice the construction: the night is described by what you do in it (rest), while the day is described by its inherent quality (giving sight). Both work together as complementary blessings.

The conclusion is striking: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ- in that, there are signs for a nation that listens. Not "a nation that sees" but a nation that listens. Why? Because the message here is about following divine guidance: the night is for rest, the day is for activity and productivity.

This carries practical implications. Allah ﷻ designed human physiology to be still at night and active during the day. The Prophet ﷺ warned about people who stay up at night because evil tends to occur under the cover of darkness. Throughout the Quran, guidance is associated with light and misguidance with darkness. This is why classical Islamic architecture was designed to allow natural light to enter from every direction, filling the space with illumination - a physical manifestation of the centrality of divine light and guidance.

The modern epidemic of staying awake late into the night, of reversing the natural order Allah ﷻ established, represents a form of not listening to these signs. Whether it's entertainment, work, or even studying Islamic knowledge - if it's happening at 2 or 3 AM regularly, we need to ask ourselves if we're among those who truly listen.

translation: They have said, "Allah has taken a son." Exalted is He; He is the [one] Free of need. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. You have no authority for this [claim]. Do you say about Allah that which you do not know
commentary:
قَالُوا۟ ٱتَّخَذَ ٱللَّهُ وَلَدًا ۗ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ ۖ هُوَ ٱلْغَنِىُّ ۖ لَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنْ عِندَكُم مِّن سُلْطَـٰنٍۭ بِهَـٰذَآ ۚ أَتَقُولُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ

Now the Quran addresses one of the most serious theological errors: the claim that Allah ﷻ has taken a son. The response begins immediately with سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ - how perfect He is, how far above and beyond such claims. The very suggestion is absurd on multiple levels.

First, هُوَ ٱلْغَنِىُّ - He is completely free of need. The concept of having children is fundamentally tied to need and dependence. Human beings have children to continue the species; without offspring, humanity would cease to exist. Every creature we see around us - elephants, bears, birds - exists only because previous generations reproduced. Having children is a form of dependence, a way for mortal beings to transcend their individual mortality.

Even the wealthiest people feel an existential sadness if they approach death without children. They scramble to adopt, to leave their inheritance to someone, to find meaning in passing something on. But attributing this need to Allah ﷻ is to fundamentally misunderstand His nature. He is الغني - the Self-Sufficient, the One who needs nothing and no one.

Moreover, لَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - He already owns everything in the heavens and the earth. The desire to have a child intensifies as people age because they want something, they want to possess something beyond themselves. But Allah ﷻ already possesses everything. The very desire for offspring implies a lack, and Allah ﷻ has no lack whatsoever.

The argument then shifts to evidence: إِنْ عِندَكُم مِّن سُلْطَـٰنٍۭ بِهَـٰذَآ - you have absolutely no authority, no proof, no evidence whatsoever for this claim. The word سلطان means authority, but here it refers to authoritative proof, evidence that compels acceptance.

The final question is devastating: أَتَقُولُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ - are you saying about Allah ﷻ what you have absolutely no knowledge of? This is the essence of the crime: speaking about the nature and attributes of the Creator without any knowledge, making claims about His essence based on conjecture and cultural tradition rather than revelation.

translation: Say, "Indeed, those who invent falsehood about Allah will not succeed
commentary:
قُلْ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ لَا يُفْلِحُونَ

The command comes to the Prophet ﷺ: قُلْ- say, declare, announce. What follows is a universal principle: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ - those who fabricate lies against Allah ﷻ, those who continuously make up falsehoods about Him.

The word يَفْتَرُونَ is in the present continuous form, indicating an ongoing action. These aren't people who made one mistake; they're persistently, habitually inventing falsehoods about Allah ﷻ and attributing them to Him.

The verdict is absolute: لَا يُفْلِحُونَ - they will not succeed, they will not prosper, they will not achieve salvation. The choice of the word فلاح here is particularly significant because it connects directly to Christian theological discourse, which was already occurring in later Makkan society. The concept of salvation, of being "saved," is central to Christian theology, and this ayah specifically addresses that audience: no matter what they claim about belief leading to salvation, those who fabricate lies about Allah ﷻ will not be saved.

translation: [For them is brief] enjoyment in this world; then to Us is their return; then We will make them taste the severe punishment because they used to disbelieve
commentary:
مَتَـٰعٌ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا ثُمَّ إِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ ثُمَّ نُذِيقُهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلشَّدِيدَ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْفُرُونَ

Whatever they enjoy is merely مَتَـٰعٌ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا - some temporary enjoyment, some passing pleasure in this worldly life. The word متاع refers to goods, provisions, temporary benefits. It's not permanent, not substantial, not real success.

ثُمَّ إِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ - then to Us alone, exclusively, is their final return. The word مرجع emphasizes that the return is inevitable and final. Everyone will come back to Allah ﷻ, and on that day, the temporary enjoyments of this world will be revealed for what they truly were: distractions from reality.

ثُمَّ نُذِيقُهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلشَّدِيدَ - then We will make them taste the severe punishment. The verb أذاق means to make someone taste, to make them experience something directly and intimately. It's not just witnessing punishment from afar; it's experiencing it fully.

The reason is stated clearly: بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْفُرُونَ - because of the disbelief they engaged in, because of their persistent rejection of truth. The past continuous tense indicates this wasn't a momentary lapse but an ongoing state of rejection.

translation: And recite to them the news of Noah, when he said to his people, "O my people, if my residence and my reminding of the signs of Allah has become burdensome upon you - then I have relied upon Allah. So resolve upon your plan and [call upon] your associates. Then let not your plan be obscure to you. Then carry it out upon me and do not give me respite
commentary:
۞ وَٱتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ نُوحٍ إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِۦ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِن كَانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكُم مَّقَامِى وَتَذْكِيرِى بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ فَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ فَأَجْمِعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَكُمْ وَشُرَكَآءَكُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَكُنْ أَمْرُكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ غُمَّةً ثُمَّ ٱقْضُوٓا۟ إِلَىَّ وَلَا تُنظِرُونِ

The command comes to the Prophet ﷺ: وَٱتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ نُوحٍ- recite to them, relate to them the story of Nuh (AS). This isn't just historical information; it's a direct parallel to the Prophet's ﷺ own situation, meant to provide comfort and guidance.

The scene opens with Nuh (AS) addressing his people: يَـٰقَوْمِ إِن كَانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكُم مَّقَامِى وَتَذْكِيرِى بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ - O my people, if my standing and my reminding you of Allah's signs has become so big, so burdensome, so offensive to you.

The word كَبُرَ means to be big, but in this context, it means to be overwhelming, too much to bear, offensive. Nuh (AS) is acknowledging that his very presence, his constant reminding, has become intolerable to them. This is after 950 years of patient calling to Allah ﷻ.

But then his tone shifts dramatically: فَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ- then I have placed my trust in Allah ﷻ alone. He's not backing down, not apologizing, not trying to make himself more palatable. Instead, he's declaring his complete reliance on Allah ﷻ.

What follows is striking in its directness: فَأَجْمِعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَكُمْ وَشُرَكَآءَكُمْ - so gather together all your planning, combine all your matters, call together your associates, your false deities, your allies. Have a complete meeting, a full council of war. The verb أجمع means to bring together completely, comprehensively.

ثُمَّ لَا يَكُنْ أَمْرُكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ غُمَّةً - then let not your plan be unclear or confusing to any of you. Make sure everyone at your meeting is on the same page, that every detail is clear, that there's no confusion about what you're going to do. The word غمة  originally refers to clouds that block sunlight, and metaphorically means confusion, perplexity, something that obscures clarity.

ثُمَّ ٱقْضُوٓا۟ إِلَىَّ وَلَا تُنظِرُونِ - then execute your plan against me, and don't give me any time, don't wait, don't delay. Just do it.

This is an astonishing moment. The common image we have of Nuh (AS) is of an endlessly patient, gentle, never-angry prophet who called people for 950 years without losing his temper. But this ayah reveals something else: there came a point when Nuh (AS) reached his limit with them and said, essentially, "Bring it. Do your worst. I have Allah ﷻ."

Why is the Prophet ﷺ being told this story? Because he needs to hear it. He's facing similar opposition, similar rejection, and he needs to know that even the great Nuh (AS) reached a point of saying, "If you're going to act against me, then go ahead." It's a reminder that there's strength in tawakkul, in complete reliance on Allah ﷻ, and that sometimes the appropriate response to persistent opposition is not endless appeasement but firm, confident standing upon the truth.

translation: And if you turn away [from my advice] then no payment have I asked of you. My reward is only from Allah, and I have been commanded to be of the Muslims
commentary:
فَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَمَا سَأَلْتُكُم مِّنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ

Nuh (AS) continues his address: فَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ- if all of you turn away from my message. He's acknowledging that they might reject everything he's saying. But then he makes a crucial point: فَمَا سَأَلْتُكُم مِّنْ أَجْرٍ - I haven't asked you for any compensation whatsoever.

This addresses an implicit accusation. When people reject a message, they often try to impugn the messenger's motives: "You're just doing this for money, for power, for personal gain." Nuh (AS) cuts through that by stating clearly that he's asked for nothing from them. His message isn't a business transaction.

إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ- my compensation is only with Allah ﷻ, exclusively. He's not working for them; he's not trying to get something from them. His reward comes from Allah ﷻ alone, which means he's accountable only to Allah ﷻ.

وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ - and I have been commanded to be from among those who submit. The word المسلمين here refers to those who surrender themselves completely to Allah ﷻ. This is his mission, his purpose: not to gain their approval or their wealth, but to fulfill the command of Allah ﷻ by submitting to Him completely.

translation: And they denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the ship and made them successors, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. Then see how was the end of those who were warned
commentary:
فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ خَلَـٰٓئِفَ وَأَغْرَقْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا ۖ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ

Despite everything Nuh (AS) said, despite his sincerity and his challenge, فَكَذَّبُوهُ - they still denied him. They called him a liar, rejected his message, refused to believe.

The divine response was decisive: فَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُ - so We rescued him. The verb نجى in the second form indicates continuous, sustained rescue. This wasn't a one-time salvation; the flood lasted for an extended period, and Allah ﷻ protected Nuh (AS) and those with him throughout the entire ordeal.

وَمَن مَّعَهُۥ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ - and those who were with him in the ship. This includes not just people but also the animals, all the believers who had accepted his call over those long centuries.

وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ خَلَـٰٓئِفَ - and We made them successors, those who remain, those who inherit the earth. This concept of خلائف has already appeared in this surah. It refers to those who are left behind to continue on, to inherit, to take over. There's a responsibility implied: they're not just survivors but inheritors of a mission.

For the Prophet ﷺ and the early Muslims hearing this, the message is clear: you too are being rejected now, but you will be the khala'if. You will inherit the earth. You will be the ones who remain and continue the mission after your enemies are gone.

وَأَغْرَقْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا - and We drowned those who had lied against Our signs, who had denied Our miraculous proofs. The punishment fits the crime: they refused to board the ark of salvation, so they were consumed by the waters.

فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ - so look, observe, consider carefully what was the outcome of those who were warned. The word منذرين emphasizes that they weren't caught off guard. They had been warned repeatedly, for centuries, but they chose to ignore the warning.

translation: Then We sent after him messengers to their peoples, and they came to them with clear proofs. But they were not to believe in that which they had denied before. Thus We seal over the hearts of the transgressors
commentary:
ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ رُسُلًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِمْ فَجَآءُوهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَمَا كَانُوا۟ لِيُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِمَا كَذَّبُوا۟ بِهِۦ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَطْبَعُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلْمُعْتَدِينَ

After the detailed story of Nuh (AS), the narrative shifts to a broader historical pattern: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ رُسُلًا - then after him, We sent many messengers. The plural رسلا indicates numerous prophets across different times and places.

The phrasing here is instructive: إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِمْ  - to their nations. Grammatically, we might expect إلى أقوامهم, using the plural form of قوم to match the plural "messengers." But instead, the singular قوم is used as though all these different nations were one nation. Why? Because despite being separated by time and geography, they all responded in the same way, as if they had learned from each other, as if they were following the same script of rejection.

فَجَآءُوهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ- and they brought them the clearest of proofs, the most obvious evidences, miracles that left no room for doubt. Despite this, فَمَا كَانُوا۟ لِيُؤْمِنُوا۟- they were not going to believe.

The explanation is devastating: بِمَا كَذَّبُوا۟ بِهِۦ مِن قَبْلُ- because of what they had lied against before, earlier. This reveals a critical spiritual principle: the first time you're exposed to guidance and reject it, you still have the ability to accept it the next time. But each rejection weakens that ability. Each time you hear the truth and turn away from it, your heart's capacity to accept truth diminishes, like a muscle that atrophies from lack of use.

Eventually, a point is reached where no amount of evidence, no miracle, no clear proof will make a difference. The heart has become so hardened, so sealed, that it's incapable of accepting guidance. This isn't arbitrary divine punishment; it's the natural consequence of persistent rejection.

كَذَٰلِكَ نَطْبَعُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلْمُعْتَدِينَ - that is how We seal, We stamp, We mark the hearts of those who transgress limits. The word طبع means to seal or stamp, and متعدين refers to those who habitually violate boundaries, who persistently go beyond the limits set by Allah ﷻ. The sealing isn't the starting point; it's the end result of a long process of willful transgression.

translation: Then We sent after them Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh and his establishment with Our signs, but they behaved arrogantly and were a criminal people
commentary:
ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِم مُّوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا مُّجْرِمِينَ

After discussing messengers in general, Allah ﷻ now focuses on one specific case that will be given more detail: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِم مُّوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ - then We sent after them Musa (AS) and Haroon (AS).

The destination is specified: إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦ- to Pharaoh and his elite assembly. The word ملأ refers to the chiefs, the nobles, the inner circle of power. Interestingly, there are two types of mala' in Pharaoh's context. Some are his generals and administrators, the actual power structure. But others are members of the enslaved Israelite community who serve as informants and enforcers, keeping their own people in check while reporting back to Pharaoh. Every oppressive system needs such collaborators from within the oppressed community itself.

بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا - with Our signs, Our clear miracles. Musa (AS) and Haroon (AS) came with undeniable proof of their prophethood.

The response: فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟- they were arrogant, they considered themselves too great to accept. The word استكبار is the tenth form, indicating they deliberately made themselves big, they actively chose arrogance. And crucially, the plural is used: all of them together, Pharaoh and his mala', united in their arrogance.

وَكَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا مُّجْرِمِينَ- and they had been a criminal nation. The past continuous tense indicates this wasn't new behavior. They already had a track record of criminality, and their rejection of Musa (AS) was simply a continuation of their established pattern.

translation: So when there came to them the truth from Us, they said, "Indeed, this is obvious magic
commentary:
فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ مِنْ عِندِنَا قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَسِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ

When truth came to them - not just any truth, but الحق, the absolute truth - and specifically مِنْ عِندِنَا, from Us, from Allah ﷻ Himself, their response was predictable: قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَسِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ- they said, "This is nothing but clear magic, obvious sorcery."

This is the classic response of those who refuse to accept truth: dismiss it as trickery, as manipulation, as false appearance. They can't deny what they saw, so they redefine it. The miracles are undeniable, so they must be magic.

translation: Moses said, "Do you say [thus] about the truth when it has come to you? Is this magic? But magicians will not succeed
commentary:
قَالَ مُوسَىٰٓ أَتَقُولُونَ لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَكُمْ ۖ أَسِحْرٌ هَـٰذَا وَلَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلسَّـٰحِرُونَ

Musa's (AS) response is direct and powerful: أَتَقُولُونَ لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَكُمْ - are you saying this about the truth when it has come to you? He's not defending himself; he's defending the truth itself. The accusation isn't an attack on him personally; it's an attack on reality, on الحق.

Then comes his question: أَسِحْرٌ هَـٰذَا - is this magic? But notice the word choice. Normally, we would expect the definite article: السحر- "the magic." Instead, it's indefinite: سحر- just "magic," any magic. He's diminishing their accusation, suggesting that even by their own logic, this doesn't fit the category of magic at all.

Then he delivers a principle that appears multiple times in the Quran: وَلَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلسَّـٰحِرُونَ - and magicians never succeed, sorcerers never prosper. No matter what tricks they pull, no matter how impressive their illusions, they ultimately fail. True success, true فلاح, is impossible through deception and trickery. This is a law of spiritual reality.

translation: They said, "Have you come to us to turn us away from that upon which we found our fathers and so that you two may have grandeur in the land? And we are not believers in you
commentary:
قَالُوٓا۟ أَجِئْتَنَا لِتَلْفِتَنَا عَمَّا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ ءَابَآءَنَا وَتَكُونَ لَكُمَا ٱلْكِبْرِيَآءُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا نَحْنُ لَكُمَا بِمُؤْمِنِينَ

Unable to defend their position intellectually, they shift to other arguments. First, tradition: أَجِئْتَنَا لِتَلْفِتَنَا عَمَّا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ ءَابَآءَنَا- have you come to turn us away from what we found our fathers committed to?

The phrase عَلَيْهِ- "upon it" - reflects a particular way Muslims speak about religious commitment, influenced by Quranic Arabic. We say someone is "upon" the deen, "upon" the sunnah, "upon" the correct aqeedah. This phrasing has seeped into Islamic English because of our familiarity with Quranic language. In standard English, we would say "committed to" their fathers' ways.

Their argument is pure appeal to tradition: our ancestors did this, so it must be right. This is one of the most common barriers to accepting truth throughout history.

Then comes the political accusation: وَتَكُونَ لَكُمَا ٱلْكِبْرِيَآءُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - and so that grandeur, greatness, dominance can be for the two of you in the land. You just want power. You want to take over. This is all a power grab disguised as religion.

This mirrors exactly what was said about Nuh (AS) earlier: that he just wanted money. Now with Musa (AS) and Haroon (AS), the accusation is political ambition. Those who reject divine guidance always try to find ulterior motives in the messenger because they cannot accept that the message itself is true.

وَمَا نَحْنُ لَكُمَا بِمُؤْمِنِينَ- and we are not believers for your sake. The addition of لكما - "for your sake" - is telling. The basic meaning is simply "we won't believe you," but by adding "for your sake," they're suggesting that believing would be doing Musa (AS) and Haroon (AS) a favor, as if faith in truth is a gift to the messenger rather than a gift to oneself.

translation: And Pharaoh said, "Bring to me every learned magician
commentary:
وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ ٱئْتُونِى بِكُلِّ سَـٰحِرٍ عَلِيمٍ

Pharaoh's response is to escalate: وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ - and Pharaoh said, ٱئْتُونِى بِكُلِّ سَـٰحِرٍ عَلِيمٍ- bring me every knowledgeable magician, every expert sorcerer.

The word عليم here means highly skilled, expert, master of the craft. This isn't about gathering random magicians; it's about assembling the elite, the best of the best. Pharaoh is organizing a formal competition, bringing in expert witnesses and judges who will evaluate the contest and declare a winner.

This parallels exactly what the Quraysh did with the Prophet ﷺ. They gathered their poets, their masters of eloquence, their critics and judges, and held competitions to try to match or exceed the Quran. The structure is identical: gather the experts, have them witness the challenge, let them pronounce judgment.

translation: So when the magicians came, Moses said to them, "Throw down whatever you will throw
commentary:
فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ

When the magicians arrived, Musa (AS) spoke to them directly: أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ- throw, cast forward whatever it is you're going to throw. Go ahead. Show me what you've got.

This is confidence born of certainty. Musa (AS) knows that what he has is from Allah ﷻ, not tricks or illusions. He's not afraid of their magic because truth cannot be threatened by falsehood, no matter how impressive the falsehood appears.

The instruction is simple and direct: do your thing. I'm ready.

translation: And when they had thrown, Moses said, "What you have brought is [only] magic. Indeed, Allah will expose its worthlessness. Indeed, Allah does not amend the work of corrupters
commentary:
فَلَمَّآ أَلْقَوْا۟ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ مَا جِئْتُم بِهِ ٱلسِّحْرُ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَيُبْطِلُهُۥٓ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُصْلِحُ عَمَلَ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ

After they threw their ropes and staffs and they appeared to be moving snakes, Musa (AS) made a declaration: مَا جِئْتُم بِهِ ٱلسِّحْرُ - whatever you have brought is the magic.

Pay attention to the grammar here. He didn't say سحر - "magic." He said السحر- "the magic," with the definite article. What you've brought is THE magic - meaning real magic, actual sorcery, as opposed to what I'm about to show you, which isn't magic at all.

Then comes his confident prediction: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَيُبْطِلُهُۥٓ - no doubt, Allah ﷻ will nullify it, will cancel it out, will make it worthless. The verb أبطل means to render something null and void, to eliminate its effect entirely, like multiplying by zero. Your impressive display will be reduced to nothing.

The reason is stated as a universal principle: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُصْلِحُ عَمَلَ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ- Allah ﷻ does not rectify, does not allow to succeed, does not give traction to the work of those who cause corruption.

Magicians aren't just called corrupt (mufsid) - they're called sources of spreading corruption (mufsideen). Their work inherently spreads fasad, and Allah ﷻ has established as a cosmic law that such work cannot ultimately succeed. It might appear impressive temporarily, but it has no lasting reality, no actual substance.

translation: And Allah will establish the truth by His words, even if the criminals dislike it
commentary:
وَيُحِقُّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْحَقَّ بِكَلِمَـٰتِهِۦ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ

The surah concludes this section with a powerful declaration: وَيُحِقُّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْحَقَّ- and Allah ﷻ will prove the truth to be truth, will establish truth as reality, will make truth manifest and undeniable.

The verb أحق means to verify something as true, to prove its truth, to establish it firmly. Allah ﷻ takes truth - which always exists as truth - and makes its truth evident to everyone, impossible to deny.

بِكَلِمَـٰتِهِۦ - by means of His words, through His speech. This has multiple layers of meaning. It refers to the Quran itself: Allah ﷻ will establish truth through revealed scripture. It refers to His divine decree and command: He makes truth prevail by His will. And it refers to His promises: what He has stated will occur exactly as He stated it.

The comprehensive implication is that truth will dominate in two realms simultaneously. In the realm of belief and understanding, the Quran will prove itself undeniably true, will expose all falsehood for what it is, will answer every objection and doubt. In the physical realm, in history and politics and society, truth will also become the dominant force. The believers will be victorious. The message will spread. Falsehood will be defeated not just intellectually but practically.

وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ - even if, no matter how much the criminals hate it, despise it, are enraged by it. The word كره indicates intense dislike, hatred, rage. The criminals - those who are guilty of spiritual and moral crimes - will hate seeing truth triumph. They'll do everything they can to prevent it. But their hatred is irrelevant. Their opposition is meaningless. Truth will prevail regardless.

This is the promise given to the Prophet ﷺ and to every believer who faces opposition: no matter what the enemies of truth do, no matter how powerful they appear, no matter how much they hate the message, Allah ﷻ will establish truth through His words. The Quran will triumph. The believers will be victorious. Truth will dominate reality itself.

translation: But no one believed Moses, except [some] youths among his people, for fear of Pharaoh and his establishment that they would persecute them. And indeed, Pharaoh was haughty within the land, and indeed, he was of the transgressors
